items.title: A 3D Body from Eight Questions – No Photo, No GPU
items.author: arkadiuss
items.summary: A 3D body model can be generated from eight questionnaire questions without using photos or GPUs. The method achieves mean mass error of 3.9 kg. It's available as a REST API.

items.title: "Plain text has been around for decades and it's here to stay." – Unsung
items.author: rbanffy
items.summary: Plain text remains relevant due to its simplicity and portability. It continues to be a powerful tool for text editing. Modern tools enhance its usability.

items.title: Iliad fragment found in Roman-era mummy
items.author: wise_blood
items.summary: A fragment of Homer's Iliad was found inside a Roman-era mummy's abdomen in Egypt. The discovery was made by a Spanish archaeological team. The papyrus fragment was part of the mummy's embalming process.

items.title: Education must go beyond the mere production of words
items.author: signor_bosco
items.summary: Education should focus on understanding and critical thinking, not just memorizing words. Learning involves deeper comprehension and application of knowledge. Effective education goes beyond rote learning to foster creativity and problem-solving.

items.title: There Will Be a Scientific Theory of Deep Learning
items.author: jamie-simon
items.summary: A comprehensive scientific theory of deep learning does not yet exist, but significant progress is being made in understanding its mechanisms. Recent advancements have begun to explain some aspects of how deep learning works.

items.title: Work with the garage door up (2024)
items.author: jxmorris12
items.summary: Working with the garage door up means openly sharing your creative process. It emphasizes transparency and progress over finished products. This approach can increase opportunities for feedback and collaboration.

items.title: Email could have been X.400 times better
items.author: maguay
items.summary: Email could have been much more efficient with X.400, but its complexity and lack of modern security features made it impractical. The protocol's cumbersome nature and susceptibility to errors hindered widespread adoption. Modern email systems have largely replaced it.

items.title: The mail sent to a video game publisher
items.author: colinprince
items.summary: A person mailed a dead fly to game publisher Panic, and a developer sent a self-addressed envelope to receive feedback on their game. These unusual mails highlight the unpredictable nature of direct communication with game companies.

items.title: MacBook Neo and how the iPad should be
items.author: jen729w
items.summary: The MacBook Neo offers a full laptop experience with multitasking and productivity apps, while the iPad is better for portability and creative tasks. For most professional and academic uses, the MacBook Neo is preferable. The iPad can still run necessary apps for many needs.

items.title: The Overtom Chess Computer Museum
items.author: semyonsh
items.summary: The Overtom Chess Computer Museum was maintained by Tom Luif, showcasing dedicated chess computers from various manufacturers. For inquiries and sales, contact www.schaakcomputers.nl. The museum featured a wide range of chess computers, including models from Novag, Mephisto, and Tandy.

items.title: I cancelled Claude: Token issues, declining quality, and poor support
items.author: y42
items.summary: Many users cancelled Anthropic's Claude due to declining performance and support issues. Usage dropped significantly, and competitors gained market share. Quality concerns and pricing were major factors.

items.title: Show HN: Browser Harness – Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task
items.author: gregpr07
items.text:
Hey HN,

We got tired of browser frameworks restricting the LLM, so we removed the framework and gave the LLM maximum freedom to do whatever it's trained on. We gave the harness the ability to self correct and add new tools if the LLM wants (is pre-trained on) that.

Our Browser Use library is tens of thousands of lines of deterministic heuristics wrapping Chrome (CDP websocket). Element extractors, click helpers, target managemenet (SUPER painful), watchdogs (crash handling, file downloa
items.summary: Browser Harness is a tool that allows large language models to complete any browser task autonomously. It is hosted on GitHub and has received significant attention. It has over 6,000 stars.

items.title: GPT-5.5
items.author: rd
items.summary: GPT-5.5 is the latest AI model from OpenAI, surpassing others in intelligence and accuracy. It has a high hallucination rate but leads in reasoning efforts. It was announced in April 2026.

items.title: Do I belong in tech anymore?
items.author: patrikcsak
items.summary: Your decision to stay in tech depends on your personal fulfillment and satisfaction. Many find it rewarding, while others leave due to burnout or misalignment with personal values. Assess your happiness and career goals.

items.title: CC-Canary: Detect early signs of regressions in Claude Code
items.author: tejpalv
items.summary: CC-Canary detects early signs of regressions in Claude Code by analyzing local logs and performance metrics. It quantifies model health and visualizes potential performance drops. Essential metrics include "Read:Edit Ratio" and "Cost per Token."

items.title: Show HN: VT Code – Rust TUI coding agent with multi-provider support
items.author: vinhnx
items.text:
Hi HN, I built VT Code, a semantic coding agent. Supports all SOTA and open sources model. Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Codex. Agent Skills, Model Context Protocol and Agent Client Protocol (ACP) ready. All open source models are support. Local inference via LM Studio and Ollama (experiment). Semantic context understanding is supported by ast-grep for structured code search and ripgrep for powered grep.

I built VT Code in Rust on Ratatui. Architecture and agent loop documented in the README and
items.summary: VT Code is a Rust-based terminal coding agent with semantic code intelligence and multi-provider support. It uses Tree-sitter and ast-grep for code parsing. Released in 2026.

items.title: Open source memory layer so any AI agent can do what Claude.ai and ChatGPT do
items.author: alash3al
items.summary: An open source memory layer enables AI agents to maintain context and recall information, similar to human memory. This layer uses knowledge graphs for better context retention. It's designed to be accessible to any AI agent.

items.title: Physicists revive 1990s laser concept to propose a next-generation atomic clock
items.author: wglb
items.summary: Physicists revived a 1990s laser concept to develop a next-generation atomic clock, promising unprecedented precision. This innovation leverages superradiant lasers, which synchronize atomic emissions for superior timekeeping. The concept was published in April 2026.

items.title: SFO Quiet Airport (2025)
items.author: CaliforniaKarl
items.summary: San Francisco Airport's Fly Quiet Program reduced daily noise by 90 minutes in 2025, improving traveler experience. The program's success was highlighted in reports and traveler feedback. The initiative continues to enhance airport operations and community relations.

items.title: Devin for Terminal
items.author: nickzana
items.summary: Devin for Terminal is an AI-powered tool integrated into the skills CLI, allowing users to add it as a supported agent. It is also available as an unofficial CLI on PyPI. Devin AI operates by executing commands entered into its terminal.

items.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro in the API
items.author: arabicalories
items.summary: As of 2026, OpenAI released GPT-5.5, available to paid subscribers, enhancing technical and scientific research workflows. GPT-5.5 is accessible via the API for developers.

items.title: ML supports existence of unrecognized transient astronomical phenomena
items.author: solarist
items.summary: Machine learning has confirmed the existence of previously unrecognized transient astronomical phenomena in historical observatory images. These transient, star-like point sources appeared and vanished over short timescales before the launch of Sputnik. This discovery was made using advanced algorithms to analyze old photographic data.

items.title: Hear your agent suffer through your code
items.author: AndrewVos
items.summary: Endless Toil is a humorous plugin that plays groan sounds as AI coding agents process increasingly complex code. It supports macOS and Linux. It requires Python 3.10+.
